narrative.headline: 2022 Honda Odyssey: electrical issues lead the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 125 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 26%, followed closely by forward collision avoidance at 22%, then the engine (12%) and power train (11%). No deaths are reported. Forward collision avoidance stands out for outcomes, tied to 4 crashes and 6 injuries, so it is worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 33 reports (26%), including 1 fire and 3 injuries. Owners describe sliding doors closing on their own, among other electrical faults.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Second most reported and the most serious by outcome: 27 reports (22%), linked to 4 crashes and 6 injuries. Worth checking carefully on a test drive.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 15 reports (12%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Owners describe sudden loss of power and warning lights while driving.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 14 reports (11%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Several involve the transmission very early in ownership.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: The fewest reports here at 10 (8%), but tied to 1 crash and 6 injuries. Owners report the airbag warning light coming on.
